entry.title: EMBL spin-off Epitome Therapeutics launches to advance precision epigenome editing | EMBL
entry.summary: EMBL spin-off Epitome Therapeutics will develop a new generation of precision medicines using epigenome editing to precisely regulate gene expression.

entry.title: At AI Summit, South Korea Outlines Its AI Future With NVIDIA and Partners
entry.summary: At this week’s AI Summit in San Francisco, South Korean President Jae Myung Lee and some of the country’s top business leaders and researchers are meeting with NVIDIA and ecosystem partners to chart Korea’s AI progress.

entry.summary: Utah’s new solar power and battery storage facility is among the largest in the region.

entry.title: EPRI Journal: Celebrating Fifty Years of the Long View - EPRI Journal
entry.summary: Share this article:By Chris Warren EPRI had a very clear mission when it was founded in 1972. Launched in response to a 1965 blackout that left around 30 million people in eight Northeastern states and two Canadian provinces without power, the formation of EPRI was an acknowledgment both of the vital importance of electricity and […]

entry.title: Riot suspends NAVI streamer ardiis for transphobic Game Changers comments
entry.summary: After transphobic comments, ardiis will be unable to co-stream VCT matches for one month, but many fans believe the punishment isn't enough.

entry.title: Editors of economics journal resign after association renews Taylor & Francis contract
entry.summary: Forty-one editors, associate editors and advisory board members resigned from their roles at the Review of Social Economy after the Association for Social Economics, which owns the journal, renewed…

entry.title: First 3D nanoscale maps of human airway epithelium reveal hidden architecture of lung defense cells
entry.summary: Researchers at Queen Mary University of London and University of Cambridge in collaboration with colleagues at HHMI Janelia and NIH, have created some of the most detailed three-dimensional maps ever generated of human airway cells, revealing how the specialized cells that protect our lungs are built and organized.

entry.title: Nanopores can activate human T cells without biochemical signals
entry.summary: Immune cells protect and heal the human body. In medicine, they are specifically activated through biochemical reactions to treat certain diseases. Researchers at the Helmholtz-Zentrum Hereon, ETH Zurich, Humboldt University of Berlin, Charité Berlin and Inselspital Bern have now discovered that immune cells also respond to the surface structure of materials—without chemistry.

entry.title: RORA Coach Midnight on facing TLPH early in MSC at EWC 2026: "Fortunate and unfortunate at the same time."
entry.summary: RORA Coach Midnight talks about the Rafaela pick and how facing TLPH early in the MSC at EWC 2026 was both "fortunate and unfortunate."

entry.title: Exclusive: A couple paid more than $800,000 for a gene-editing therapy for their daughter. She died, and it wasn’t made public
entry.summary: Illustration: Daria Lada An investigation by Science and Retraction Watch has uncovered details about a clinical trial that resulted in the death of its sole patient: a 6-year-old girl with a rare …

entry.title: What Americans think about the global AI race
entry.summary: Most Americans see China as more advanced than the U.S. in AI development, while 43% say U.S. leadership in AI is extremely or very important.

entry.title: As the 2026 Midterms Approach, Economy Is Front and Center
entry.summary: Americans are now almost evenly split over which party they most agree with on economic policies: 37% say the Democrats, while 36% say the GOP.

It’s time for soundproof booths to return to esports
entry.summary: After a Call of Duty Grand Final was heavily influenced by crowd behavior, we need to bring back soundproof booths in esports.

entry.summary: NVIDIA OptiX ray tracing engine is an application framework for achieving optimal ray tracing performance on the GPU. Applications using OptiX can fail in ways that are difficult to diagnose: an…

entry.title: "Keep fighting for your dream": Team Vitality MLBB pro Vivian on overcoming challenges and judgement to become a top esports pro
entry.summary: Team Vitality Mobile Legends: Bang Bang (MLBB) player Vivi "Vivian" Indrawaty changed her life and her family's by banking on her esports career.

entry.title: Lipid nanoparticles deliver one-two punch to shrink resistant oral tumors in preclinical models
entry.summary: Oral squamous cell carcinoma (OSCC) is the most common form of head and neck cancer, and the number of new cases is predicted to rise by 30% in the next decade. Despite decades of cancer research, treating OSCC remains a challenge. The five-year survival rate is approximately 50%, and standard treatments such as ablative surgery and radiation often leave patients with permanent disfigurement or profound, lifelong impairment of essential oral functions such as speaking and swallowing.

entry.title: Start Customizing NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano with Prime Intellect Lab in Minutes | NVIDIA Technical Blog
entry.summary: Customization is what enables developers to take a general model and tailor it to use cases, domains, languages, and more. However, customization comes with a few challenges.

entry.title: PlayVS takes esports to 50,000 Scouts at the 2026 National Jamboree
entry.summary: PlayVS is rolling out a dedicated gaming arena at Scouting America's biggest youth gathering, running July 22–31 in West Virginia — part of a broader push to plant esports in unexpected spaces.

entry.title: Combining AI with laboratory evolution yields improved proteins
entry.summary: Researchers found that AI-redesigned versions of natural proteins serve as superior starting points to evolve proteins with new functions.

entry.summary: BGS is part of a new, collaborative project, funded through NERC’s ‘Opening up the environment’ programme, helping the environmental research community become more accessible.

entry.summary: Esports Foundation's Hans Jagnow talks about the Esports World Cup's relocation, the changing perception of esports, and esports fandom.

entry.title: Risk without reward: Counter-Strike 2's new sticker system threatens the entire esports scene's structure
entry.summary: Valve just made some big changes to the sticker system, causing Counter-Strike 2 teams to lose a ton of revenue. This could spell trouble.

entry.summary: Snow-capped mountains carved by deep river valleys preside over northwestern Washington state.

entry.summary: New data tables released about employment, social connectedness, and other topics from the experimental HTOPS.

entry.title: The Census Bureau Higher Education Consortium: Transforming Collaboration Between the U.S. Census Bureau and Higher Education
entry.summary: The CBHEC is a collaborative effort by the Census Bureau and academia to develop innovators to meet the nation’s statistical needs.

entry.title: NVIDIA AI Supercomputer Comes Online at Naval Postgraduate School
entry.summary: NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang joined school leadership in Monterey, California, to commission the system, which will fuel AI education and research at the US military’s premier technical graduate university.

entry.title: Reusable magnetic invention removes microplastics plus some PFAS from water
entry.summary: Microplastics are an increasing global concern, with growing evidence of their presence in water systems. RMIT University researchers have developed a water treatment material that rapidly removes micro- and nanoplastics and some PFAS (per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances), bringing the technology closer to real-world use. The invention builds on the team's 2022 breakthrough in microplastics removal, extending its performance to much smaller particles and more complex wastewater.

entry.title: Forget the burrito bowl: Karmine Corp's Blue Rising TCG is the future of esports monetization
entry.summary: Sick of the boring slapped on logos and lazy sponsored social media posts? Karmine Corp is showing how esports can truly thrive.

entry.title: Lich Queen Mei rises in new Overwatch x World of Warcraft collaboration!
entry.summary: The legendary Lich Queen Mei skin and Frostmourne Ana weapon have entered Overwatch as part of the game's latest World of Warcraft crossover.

entry.title: New optical method follows single proteins as they shift shape
entry.summary: Researchers at the University of Twente have developed an optical method that follows the shape changes of a single protein in liquid without attaching anything to it. It reads the protein's structure from its own molecular vibrations, free from the labels or tags that other techniques rely on. The method could help researchers study how proteins respond to drugs, toxins and other biomolecules. The paper is published in the journal ACS Nano.

entry.title: Robotic Servicing Mission Launches with NASA Support - NASA

entry.title: Faker named Youth Cyber Gambling Awareness Ambassador by Korean National Police Agency
entry.summary: Faker will front a nationwide campaign in South Korea, encouraging young people struggling with cyber gambling to seek help.

entry.title: Combined nanoparticle and internal laser method could make cancer therapy less invasive
entry.summary: In a promising development for cancer treatment, a collaborative research team has overcome two issues that have prevented photothermal therapy, a much less invasive treatment option than surgery and/or radiation, from becoming more common.

entry.title: Berkeley Lab to Lead 13 New Genesis Mission AI Projects
entry.summary: Researchers will apply AI, supercomputing, and advanced instruments to accelerate discoveries for energy, science, and national security.

entry.title: U.S. Department of Energy invests in Fermilab projects to accelerate AI-enabled scientific discovery
entry.summary: The awards will support Fermilab projects by leveraging AI to boost innovation and technology development, advance DOE’s Genesis Mission and accelerate scientific discovery.

entry.title: FTC Returns Money to Consumers Harmed by Trend Deploy’s Deceptive Marketing
entry.summary: The Federal Trade Commission is sending more than $672,000 to consumers deceived by the operator of Trend Deploy, Frank Romero.
